feed.title: Matthew Feil
feed.subtitle: The website of Matthew Feil, a high school student with a passion for music and technology.

entry.title: Chicago ‘L’ Live Art
entry.content:
Try Chicago ‘L’ Live Art here!

Hi! I made Chicago ‘L’ Live Art using live Chicago ‘L’ train data. Instead of making a normal train tracker with a map, I wanted it to feel more like an abstract piece of art that happens to be powered by real live information. The page gets train positions from the CTA Train Tracker API, then turns each train’s latitude and longitude into a position on a grid. From there, the website draws the trains as moving pixels on the screen.

Each colored square represents a train, and the color matches the CTA line it is on. When trains are moving, the page shows a blinking or pulsing effect to indicate the direction the train is moving. If multiple trains end up on the same grid square, the color can shift or blink between them.

The settings let you change the look without changing the live data behind it:

  • Standard mode shows the normal moving live view
  • Still mode makes the trains feel more like a static light grid
  • Smooth mode blends movement more gradually between positions
  • Pixel size changes how large or small the grid blocks are
  • Normal, pastel, and monochrome color modes change the palette
  • The line toggles let you choose which CTA lines appear

This project is meant to use live data for a new purpose to create a cool design; you might even call it a work of art. Hope you enjoy!

entry.title: Investment Calculators
entry.content:
Try the stock calculator here!

Hi! Many people are aware of the possible earnings from investing, but yet spend money instead of saving for the future. Of course you can do whatever you want with your own money, but a lot of people don’t realize the actual value of their money if saved and invested for the future. I made a calculator to show you what you would have earned if you invested either in the stock market or in a savings account/CD with interest. Simply input the start/end date, initial dollar amount, and, for the stock calculator, the ticker of what you want to track (it defaults to the S&P 500). The calculator even accounts for interest. Of course this simply shows past information and doesn’t guarantee future gains. I simply intend for this to be educational and show you the potential value of money of saved. Hope you enjoy!

entry.title: Improved Grade Calculator
entry.content:
Try the improved calculator here!

It’s that time of year: finals season. It can be pretty stressful for students, but what makes it even worse is not knowing what grade you’ll end up with after you take the final. A year ago, I made a grade calculator to help students calculate what grade they would need to earn on the final to get a certain letter grade for their total grade. It’s pretty useful, however it was pretty limited. You were stuck with one set grading scale as well as only rounding when 0.5% or less away. I’ve made an improved version with a few more features, hopefully to make figuring out grades a little easier. I hope you find it helpful, and good luck on your finals!

What’s new?

  • Instant results (no need to press calculate)
  • Toggleable rounding on/off
  • Customizable rounding percentage (within the nearest x%)
  • Customizable grading scale (percentage to letter grade conversion)
  • Customizable final to semester grade weighing ratio
  • Enter a grade you think you’ll get on the final and see what grade it’ll earn you

entry.title: Finals Grade Calculator
entry.content:
Try the calculator here!

It’s almost time for finals! Many students including myself will be studying hard, trying to get the best grades they can. But sometimes you don’t have enough time to study for everything, and you have to decide what to study and what to skip. It can be helpful know what grade you will end up with after the final, because a 97% and a 93% (both A’s) have the same GPA. My school gives a confusing and repetitive equation students can use to calculate their final grade, with the semester grade weighing 80% and the final exam 20%. I decided to see if ChatGPT could make a website to calculate your grades easily. It worked suprisingly well. So here’s the website it made with minimal revision by myself. All you have to do is input your current semester grade, and it will tell you what you need to get on the final for each letter grade, and it will also say if the grade is impossible or guarnteed!
